prompt:
Passage 1
A distribution center recently adopted handheld scanning devices to track inventory. Previously, employees recorded product information on paper forms and later entered the data into a computer system. Managers found that the process was time-consuming and occasionally resulted in data-entry errors.
With the new devices, employees scan barcodes as products move through the facility. The information is transmitted directly to the inventory database. Supervisors report that inventory records are updated more quickly, and employees spend less time completing paperwork.
The company also uses the system to identify trends in product movement. By reviewing the data, managers can determine which items are shipped most frequently and adjust storage locations accordingly. Although training was required before implementation, most employees adapted to the new system within a few weeks.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: The company plans to eliminate inventory tracking.
choices.B: A distribution center improved inventory management by adopting scanning technology.
choices.D: Paper records are more accurate than digital records.

explanation: The passage focuses on how a distribution center improved inventory tracking and efficiency by adopting handheld scanning devices. The other choices are not supported by the passage.

prompt:
Passage 2
NOTICE
Beginning August 1, the company cafeteria will operate under revised hours. Breakfast service will be available from 6:00 a.m. to 8:30 a.m., and lunch service will be available from 11:00 a.m. to 1:30 p.m.
The changes were made after a review of customer traffic patterns. Data showed that cafeteria usage outside these periods was very low. Concentrating service during peak hours will allow staff to improve efficiency while continuing to meet employee needs.
Employees are encouraged to provide feedback during the first month of the new schedule.

Why were the cafeteria hours changed?

choices.A: The cafeteria is being permanently closed.

explanation: The notice explains that cafeteria hours were changed after a review of customer traffic patterns.

prompt:
Passage 3
Many cities have installed electronic message boards along major roads. These signs can display information about traffic conditions, construction zones, weather-related hazards, and emergency situations.
One advantage of electronic message boards is flexibility. Unlike traditional printed signs, electronic displays can be updated quickly as conditions change. Drivers can receive current information that may help them avoid delays or hazardous situations.
Transportation officials note that the effectiveness of these signs depends on clear communication. Messages must be concise enough for drivers to read safely while still providing useful information.
As technology improves, some transportation agencies are exploring ways to integrate message boards with other traffic-management systems to improve the flow of information.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: Electronic message boards provide drivers with updated information.
choices.B: Traditional signs are illegal in most cities.
choices.C: Road construction causes all traffic delays.
choices.D: Weather forecasting is becoming less important.

explanation: The passage explains how electronic message boards provide updated information to drivers and discusses their benefits.

prompt:
Passage 4
In the early days of commercial refrigeration, businesses faced significant challenges when transporting food over long distances. Perishable products often spoiled before reaching customers, limiting the size of potential markets.
The development of reliable refrigerated transportation changed this situation dramatically. Railcars, trucks, and later shipping containers equipped with refrigeration systems allowed products to remain fresh for longer periods. Businesses gained access to wider markets, and consumers had access to a greater variety of foods throughout the year.
Today, refrigerated transportation remains an important part of global supply chains. Companies continue to invest in technologies that improve temperature control and monitoring during shipment.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: Food transportation is no longer necessary.
choices.B: Refrigerated transportation transformed the movement of perishable goods.
choices.C: Railroads are the only method of shipping food.

explanation: The passage explains how refrigerated transportation transformed the movement and distribution of perishable goods.

prompt:
Passage 5
A large office building recently introduced a digital visitor management system. Previously, guests signed a paper logbook when entering the building. Security personnel then manually notified employees that their visitors had arrived.
Under the new system, visitors enter their information on a touchscreen kiosk located in the lobby. The system prints a temporary identification badge and automatically sends a notification to the employee being visited. Building management reports that check-in times have decreased and recordkeeping has become more accurate.
The system also allows security staff to quickly identify who is in the building during an emergency. While some visitors required assistance during the first few weeks, usage became smoother as people became familiar with the process.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: The building eliminated all security procedures.
choices.B: A digital visitor management system improved building operations.
choices.D: Paper records are required by law.

explanation: The passage focuses on the implementation of a digital visitor management system and its benefits.

prompt:
Passage 6
Many companies use preventive maintenance programs to reduce equipment breakdowns. Instead of waiting for machinery to fail, technicians inspect, clean, lubricate, and service equipment according to a planned schedule.
Supporters of preventive maintenance argue that routine service helps identify problems before they become serious. A worn component, for example, may be replaced during a scheduled inspection rather than after it causes a shutdown.
Although preventive maintenance requires time and resources, many organizations believe the investment is worthwhile because it reduces unexpected downtime and extends equipment life.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: Equipment should only be repaired after failure.
choices.B: Preventive maintenance helps reduce equipment problems and downtime.
choices.D: Equipment replacement is always less expensive than maintenance.

explanation: The passage explains how preventive maintenance helps reduce equipment breakdowns and downtime.

choices.A: Waiting for equipment to fail before taking action

explanation: Preventive maintenance involves inspecting and servicing equipment according to a planned schedule.

prompt:
Passage 7
For decades, weather forecasting relied heavily on observations collected from ground stations, ships, and weather balloons. While these sources provided valuable information, coverage was limited in some regions.
The introduction of weather satellites greatly expanded the amount of data available to meteorologists. Satellites can monitor cloud patterns, storm development, and atmospheric conditions across large areas. This information is combined with data from traditional sources to create forecasts.
Although forecasts are not always perfect, advances in technology have generally improved forecasting accuracy. Better forecasts can help individuals, businesses, and emergency planners make informed decisions.

What is the passage mainly about?

choices.A: Why weather forecasting should be discontinued

explanation: The passage primarily discusses how weather satellites improved forecasting by increasing available data.

prompt:
Passage 8
A city transportation department recently completed a study of bicycle commuting patterns. The study examined how frequently residents used bicycles for travel to work and which factors influenced participation.
Researchers found that bicycle commuting was more common in areas with connected bike lanes and secure bicycle parking. Survey responses also indicated that many commuters valued predictable travel times and reduced transportation costs.
Based on the findings, city officials announced plans to expand sections of the bicycle network and install additional parking facilities. Officials stated that the goal is to provide residents with more transportation options rather than replace existing modes of travel.

What was the purpose of the study?

choices.A: To determine how residents commute by bicycle and what affects participation

explanation: The study examined bicycle commuting rates and the factors influencing participation.
